body:
Bead: agent-cb608a4f — Step 8a — Adapter: pi Outcome: no changes (blocked on prerequisite) What is done ------------ - Read the bead contract, SD-010 §4 (normative adapter contract), the harness-matrix-plan v7 (Step 3 + Step 8), and the NEW14 install spike (docs/research/oss-harness-install-2026-04-29.md). Pi is KEPT and a clear install/flag/profile mapping exists in that spike. - Verified the worktree state: * `scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/` does NOT exist. * No `base.py`, no `Agent`/`BaseInstalledAgent` shim, no shared `_test/fake_provider.py` helper anywhere in the tree. * `.ddx/beads.jsonl` shows agent-86dc6092 ("Step 3 — Adapter base + ddx-agent + noop + dumb_script + FakeProvider", a.k.a. NEW9) is still `open` — it has not been claimed or closed. What is blocking ---------------- This bead's description is explicit that pi.py implements the contract "declared in NEW9 base.py" and that "Calibration matrix from NEW9 still passes alongside". Both prerequisites are unmet: 1. There is no base.py to subclass. SD-010 §4.1 specifies the protocol (`class Agent(BaseInstalledAgent)` with install / command / apply_profile / parse_telemetry / redact_secrets), but the concrete `BaseInstalledAgent` shim — which Harbor's adapter loader binds to — is owned by NEW9. Implementing it here would either (a) duplicate a contract NEW9 will land days later and force a reconciling rewrite, or (b) silently fold NEW9's scope into this bead, violating the single-bead boundary. 2. There is no shared `FakeProvider`. SD-010 §4.2 states the helper "is shared across adapter tests, not reimplemented per adapter", and NEW9's AC includes shipping it. Writing a pi-local fake here would guarantee a duplicate / divergent helper when NEW9 lands. 3. The bead AC line "Calibration matrix from NEW9 still passes alongside" cannot be evaluated: the calibration matrix (noop + dumb_script adapters under harness_adapters/) does not yet exist. What a follow-up attempt would need ----------------------------------- - NEW9 (agent-86dc6092) closed first, landing: * scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/base.py with the BaseInstalledAgent superclass + telemetry/redaction helpers * scripts/benchmark/harness_adapters/_test/fake_provider.py * noop + dumb_script + ddx_agent reference adapters and their tests * a way to run the calibration matrix locally so this bead can prove "still passes alongside" - Once base.py exists, this bead is a straightforward translation of docs/research/oss-harness-install-2026-04-29.md §pi into pi.py: * install: `npm install -g @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent@0.67.1` * command: `pi --mode json --print --no-session --no-extensions --no-skills --no-prompt-templates --no-themes --provider

--model [--thinking ] ""` with stdin=/dev/null * apply_profile: route openai-compat profiles via PI_OPENAI_COMPAT_BASE_URL + PI_OPENAI_COMPAT_API_KEY; map sampling.reasoning → --thinking; surface adapter_translation_notes for the un-pluggable sampling.temperature and limits.max_output_tokens fields. * parse_telemetry: parse the existing pi `--mode json` event stream (the same shape internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go already consumes via runStreaming, internal/harnesses/pi/runner.go:171) into the D4 schema; tool_call_errors and cached_input_tokens emit `null` when not surfaced. * redact_secrets: scrub PI_OPENAI_COMPAT_API_KEY plus the value of whatever env name profile.provider.api_key_env points at. - pytest test file mirrors NEW9's FakeProvider pattern; asserts argv shape, env exports, telemetry parse on a captured fixture, and secret scrubbing. Per the bead's audit-trail clause, the alternative resolution (close-with-reason=dropped) does NOT apply here: the NEW14 spike explicitly KEPT pi (status: KEPT — proceed to Step 8 adapter (NEW15)). The block is sequencing, not scope. rationale: Bead: agent-cb608a4f — Step 8a — Adapter: pi Outcome: no changes (blocked on prerequisite) What is done ------------ - Read the bead contract, SD-010 §4 (normative adapter contract), the harness-matrix-plan v7 (Step 3 + Step 8), and the NEW14 install spike (docs/research/oss-harness-install-2026-04-29.md). The block is sequencing, not scope. result_rev=95f5a6ad6e054a06f1e91aa44073a6ecabe33e78 base_rev=95f5a6ad6e054a06f1e91aa44073a6ecabe33e78
